questions.subject: Anatomy
questions.explanation: The phrenic nerve (C3-C5) provides the sole motor supply to the diaphragm.

questions.subject: Pharmacology
questions.explanation: Intramuscular adrenaline is first-line because it rapidly treats airway edema, bronchospasm, and hypotension.

questions.subject: Pathology
questions.explanation: Reed-Sternberg cells are large binucleate cells classically associated with Hodgkin lymphoma.

questions.subject: Physiology
questions.prompt: Resting membrane potential of a neuron is closest to which value?
questions.explanation: A typical neuron rests near -70 mV, largely set by potassium permeability and the sodium-potassium pump.

questions.subject: Anatomy
questions.prompt: Artery most commonly occluded in an inferior myocardial infarction?
questions.explanation: The right coronary artery supplies the inferior wall in most people, so inferior MIs usually involve the RCA.

questions.subject: Physiology
questions.explanation: Parathyroid hormone raises calcium via bone resorption, renal calcium reabsorption, and activation of vitamin D.
